prompt: Within the 'Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Liverpool FC' event, what are the current implied probabilities, best bids, best asks, and spreads for 'Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?', 'Will Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Liverpool FC end in a draw?', and 'Will Liverpool FC win on 2026-04-08?'?
whyUsersCare: A buyer wants the whole live same-event quote board in one answer, not three separate contract lookups.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A generic model cannot inspect current same-event Polymarket quotes and spreads for all three outcomes.

groundedEntity.title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Liverpool FC

answerabilityNote: Gamma `/events/slug/{slug}` or contributor `get_event_by_slug` returns the event markets and token IDs; `compare_event_outcome_quotes` can then compute per-outcome `currentPrice`, `bestBid`, `bestAsk`, and `spread` from live quote data.

prompt: For the 'Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?' market, how much merged depth is available right now and what slippage would a $1k, $5k, and $10k exit face?
whyUsersCare: Execution quality matters more than the headline probability when trading real size into a live event market.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A free model cannot inspect the current CLOB book or compute live exit slippage from it.

groundedEntity.title: Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?

answerabilityNote: Resolve the market via Gamma `/markets/slug/{slug}` or event markets from `/events/slug/{slug}` to get the token ID, then use merged CLOB book data. `analyze_market_liquidity` exposes `bestBid`, `bestAsk`, depth, and `whaleCost.sell1k/sell5k/sell10k`.

prompt: Within the '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner ' event, compare Spain, France, and England on current implied probability, current spread, and one-week price change.
whyUsersCare: Same-event cross-outcome comparison is how a trader spots which favorites are rich, cheap, or simply easier to trade.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot fuse live same-event quote data with current venue-specific price history.
answerabilityNote: Gamma `/events/slug/{slug}` or `search_and_get_outcomes` provides the event outcomes and token IDs; `compare_event_outcome_quotes` joins current quote fields with `get_price_history` summary fields over `interval=1w`.

prompt: Within the '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner ' event, which sampled outcomes currently look most tradable and which look headline-only among Spain, France, England, Switzerland, and South Korea?
whyUsersCare: Large multi-outcome boards hide big differences between visible prices and actually tradable markets.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Generic models cannot rank live event outcomes by spread, depth, slippage, and market quality.
answerabilityNote: `rank_event_tradability` resolves the event via Gamma event endpoints, then ranks named outcomes using live quote/liquidity fields such as `spreadCents`, `totalDepthUsd`, and `slippage5kPercent`.

prompt: Within the '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner ' event, which sampled outcome has the strongest whale backing right now among Spain, France, England, Switzerland, New Zealand, and South Korea?
whyUsersCare: Buyers want to know where large wallets are concentrating across a crowded event board before trusting a favorite's price.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A free model cannot inspect current whale exposure across live sibling contracts in one event.
answerabilityNote: `analyze_event_whale_breakdown` resolves the event from Gamma discovery data and scans holder surfaces across outcome markets; it returns `whalesByOutcome[].totalWhaleValue`, `topWhalePosition`, and `whaleCount`.

prompt: Within the 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?' event, which date buckets are taking the biggest share of live trading right now, especially 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 15?' versus 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 30?' and 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by June 30?'
whyUsersCare: Crowded event ladders are only useful if you can see which buckets are actually attracting live flow right now.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot rank current event submarkets by live volume share inside the same Polymarket family.

groundedEntity.title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?

answerabilityNote: `get_event_by_slug` returns the numeric `event.id` plus child `markets[].conditionId`; `get_event_live_volume` then uses live volume fields `total`, `markets[].value`, and `markets[].shareOfEventTotal` to rank active buckets.

prompt: For the 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 15?' market, what are the current best bid, best ask, midpoint, spread, and vig-adjusted implied probabilities?
whyUsersCare: A trader needs the live execution picture plus true implied odds before trusting a geopolitical time-bucket contract.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Generic models do not have the current book, midpoint, or vig-adjusted live probabilities for this contract.

groundedEntity.title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 15?

answerabilityNote: Gamma `/markets/slug/{slug}` or contributor resolution gives the market/conditionId; `check_market_efficiency` uses live outcome prices plus quote data to return `bestBid`, `bestAsk`, `midPrice`, `spread`, `vig`, and `trueProbabilities`.

prompt: For the 'Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?' market, what exactly makes the contract resolve YES or NO, and what are the main resolution gotchas?
whyUsersCare: Resolution semantics can dominate expected value on politically charged contracts.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Search results may discuss the topic, but they do not expose the exact live contract wording and current venue metadata.

groundedEntity.title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?

answerabilityNote: `check_market_rules` reads market metadata surfaced from Gamma market/event endpoints and returns rule-bearing fields like `description`, `resolutionSource`, `rulesSummary.resolvesYesIf`, `rulesSummary.resolvesNoIf`, and `potentialGotchas`.

prompt: For the 'Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be Viktor Orbán?' market, are top holders and recent trade flow aligned on YES or NO, or is there whale-versus-retail divergence?
whyUsersCare: Holder concentration and flow divergence can show whether a price is broadly supported or whale-driven.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A free model cannot inspect current holder concentration and live trade-size buckets for this exact market.

groundedEntity.title: Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be Viktor Orbán?

answerabilityNote: `analyze_single_market_whales` combines recent trade data with holder scans for one live market and returns smart-money, flow-by-size, and divergence outputs grounded in current market data.

prompt: For the 'Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be Péter Magyar?' market, how concentrated is the holder base and how much of each side is controlled by the top whales?
whyUsersCare: A concentrated book behaves differently from one supported by broad participation.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Generic models cannot inspect live per-side concentration and whale counts on the current venue.

groundedEntity.title: Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be Péter Magyar?

answerabilityNote: `analyze_top_holders` or `get_top_holders` can answer this from holder surfaces; the contributor returns concentration fields like `top5YesPercent`, `top5NoPercent`, `whaleCount`, and `totalUniqueHolders`.

prompt: Inside the 'The Masters - Winner ' event, compare Xander Schauffele, Cameron Young, and Akshay Bhatia on current price, spread, and which one is easiest to exit size in.
whyUsersCare: Visible outrights are not equally tradable, especially when a buyer wants to move size.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A free model cannot compare current same-event spread and exit-liquidity conditions across live golfer contracts.
answerabilityNote: Gamma `/events/slug/{slug}` provides the event outcomes and token IDs; `compare_event_outcome_quotes` covers pricing/spreads and `analyze_event_outcome_liquidity` adds `totalDepthUsd` and slippage fields.

prompt: For the 'Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?' market, what are the live trading parameters on the token, including tick size, fee rate, minimum order size, and whether negative risk is enabled?
whyUsersCare: Tick size, fees, and order minimums materially affect how tradable a market is in practice.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A generic model cannot inspect current venue-native trading parameters for the resolved token.

groundedEntity.title: Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?

answerabilityNote: Use Gamma `/events/slug/{slug}` or `/markets/slug/{slug}` to resolve the market and token, then `get_market_parameters` returns `tickSize`, `feeRateBps`, `minOrderSize`, and `negRisk`.

prompt: For the 'Will Stephen A. Smith win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?' market, does the contract look rich or cheap relative to Gretchen Whitmer and Oprah Winfrey in the same 'Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028' event right now?
whyUsersCare: Relative pricing across sibling contracts is more useful than any one headline probability in isolation.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot compare current same-event quote structure across these exact live contracts.

groundedEntity.title: Will Stephen A. Smith win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?

answerabilityNote: `compare_market_against_related_contracts` resolves the parent event from the named market via Gamma event/market discovery, then compares sibling outcomes using live fields like `currentPrice`, `spread`, and `eventPriceSum`.

prompt: For the 'Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?' market, what do recent trades, current open interest, and the open-interest-to-volume ratio say about whether positioning looks sticky or just churn?
whyUsersCare: Tape plus open interest reveals whether a move is real positioning or just churn around a macro headline.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A free model cannot inspect current venue-specific trade history and open-interest state for this contract.

groundedEntity.title: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?

answerabilityNote: `summarize_live_market_activity` uses recent trade records plus open-interest snapshot fields to interpret whether positioning looks sticky or noisy.

prompt: Build a buy, speculative buy, pass, or avoid memo for the 'Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?' market that combines rules risk, quote quality, whale positioning, and recent tape.
whyUsersCare: The most valuable answer is a decision-ready memo that fuses multiple live venue-native signals into one view.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A generic model cannot produce a grounded memo that combines current rules, quotes, whales, and recent activity for this contract.

groundedEntity.title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?

answerabilityNote: `build_market_tradability_memo` combines Gamma rule metadata, live quote/liquidity fields, whale signals, and recent activity into a final `decision` and `memo`.

prompt: Within the 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?' event, which currently live bucket offers the best mix of clean rules, tradable quotes, and whale support among 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 15?', 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 30?', and 'Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by June 30?'
whyUsersCare: This is the kind of same-event trade selection workflow a paying buyer actually wants solved end to end.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot combine current rules, quotes, and whale support across several live sibling contracts.

groundedEntity.title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?

answerabilityNote: Gamma `/events/slug/{slug}` supplies sibling markets and token IDs; quote tools provide `currentPrice`/`spread`, whale tools provide cross-outcome holder signals, and rule tools expose `resolutionSource` and gotchas.

prompt: Within the 'US x Iran ceasefire by...?' event, which future buckets are still live after the expired dates, and among the live buckets which one currently has the highest quoted probability and the most volume?
whyUsersCare: Buyers need to know which contracts in a time-bucket ladder are still relevant and live before doing deeper analysis.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: Generic models cannot inspect the current live/expired state and quote board of a contract family from the venue.
answerabilityNote: Gamma `/events/slug/{slug}` returns child markets with `question`, `outcomePrices`, `volume`, `endDate`, and active/closed status. Contributor event tools can filter live buckets and rank by price and volume.

prompt: Inside the 'Thunder vs. Clippers' event, which submarkets are actually attracting live trading right now: the main 'Thunder vs. Clippers' moneyline, 'Thunder vs. Clippers: O/U 227.5', or props like 'Kris Dunn: Points O/U 5.5'?
whyUsersCare: A live board is only useful if you can tell whether the action is in the core market, totals, or props.
whyWebSearchBaselineWeak: A baseline model cannot compare live event submarket activity shares across moneyline, totals, and props.
answerabilityNote: `get_event_by_slug` provides the event id and child `conditionId`s, while `get_event_live_volume` returns per-submarket `markets[].value` and `shareOfEventTotal` for current activity comparisons.
